document_id: SUPLR 2012-0022 Sales of Medical Items and Instructional Materials

findings.description: State seal/logo is repositioned and reduced in prominence in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1 of the PDF, the circular seal is centered above the department heading; in the HTML it appears smaller and shifted toward the upper left above the heading.

findings.description: Header formatting is altered, with the main title left-aligned and restyled.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows 'North Carolina Department of Revenue' centered near the top with governor/secretary names flanking it; the HTML renders the title left-aligned with a horizontal rule beneath and the side names stacked on the left.

findings.description: Top-of-page layout is simplified and redistributed in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 1 uses a centered letterhead structure with left and right header blocks and broad whitespace; the HTML consolidates these into a more linear single-column arrangement, though the main body content remains intact.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated theme/CSS content and metadata not present in the PDF, including branding text and external font import.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains text such as "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", CSS variables like "--nc-navy", and "@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Sans+3...')". None of this content appears anywhere in the PDF pages.

findings.description: Heading structure does not reflect the source document structure; the body content is mostly a letter with no semantic headings, but the HTML promotes the agency name to an h1.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses "

North Carolina Department of Revenue

" as a document title. In the PDF, this appears as letterhead text centered under the seal, not a section heading or title for the ruling body text.

findings.description: Image alt text is acceptable but slightly interpretive rather than purely literal.
findings.evidence: HTML image uses alt="North Carolina state seal". The PDF shows the state seal graphic at the top center. This is broadly accurate, though the exact wording is added by the converter.
